untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tick-direction detection (strict downtick vs uptick vs zero-tick discrimination) and dynamic limit price adjustment logic. These are order execution mechanics not representable as price/indicator conditions. The 'tick_type_buy', 'tick_type_sell', 'zero_tick_inclusion', and 'limit_price_adjustment' parameters require tick-level order management outside standard OHLCV indicators.
translation_notes: Translated directional tick conditions using close vs prev_close as proxy for uptick/downtick. Entry conditions represent buy on downtick (close < prev_close) and sell on uptick (close > prev_close). Exit conditions reverse on tick direction change. However, the core signal intent around dynamic limit price latching and order-level tick discrimination cannot be fully captured in indicator-based rules and requires implementation at the order execution layer.
